title: ericameria nauseosa var. hololeuca
intro: Ericameria nauseosa var. hololeuca, also known as Holyleaved Rubber Rabbitbrush, is a variety of shrub native to the western United States and Canada. This plant is a member of the sunflower family and has been used for its medicinal and health benefits for centuries. The leaves and flowers of the Holyleaved Rubber Rabbitbrush contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support skin health.
